criteria.label: Compute E[X] and Var(X) for a discrete distribution from its PMF.
Apply linearity of expectation to simplify E[aX + bY + c].
criteria.example: With E[X]=2, E[Y]=5, find E[3X − 2Y + 1] = 3·2 − 2·5 + 1 = −3 without knowing whether X and Y are independent.

criteria.label: Use Var(X) = E[X²] − (E[X])² and scaling rule Var(aX + b) = a² Var(X).
criteria.example: If Var(X)=9, compute Var(2X − 7) = 4·9 = 36; explain that adding constant −7 does not change variance.

criteria.label: Compute sample mean and unbiased sample variance from data with NumPy.
criteria.example: For data [10, 12, 14, 18], report x̄=13.5 and s² with ddof=1 equal to 14.667 (or s≈3.83).

criteria.label: Explain why Var(X + Y) ≠ Var(X) + Var(Y) in general and when variances add.
criteria.example: State Var(X+Y)=Var(X)+Var(Y)+2Cov(X,Y); give the Y=X case where Var(2X)=4Var(X), and note variances add when Cov=0.

criteria.label: Connect MSE/RMSE to expected squared error in regression evaluation.
criteria.example: Argue that minimizing MSE targets E[Y|X] and that validation RMSE is √(average squared residual), linking model quality to variance of errors.
